active_work.next_action: R&B deep review (docs/references/ has PDFs). Then TASKS.md generation, then Stage 3C planning.

orientation.core_insight: sigma-TAP is not a standard agent-based model. It models PRAXIS (how agents become through action) using the L-matrix as its fundamental accounting unit. Every interaction is classified into one of four channels (L11=self-self, L12=self-other, L21=other-self, L22=env-env). The philosophical sources (Levinas, Sartre, D&G, Emery-Trist) are not decoration — they independently converge on this same 4-channel structure from different directions, which is the user's key claim.
orientation.practical_warning: Do NOT introduce novel claims or impose standard ABM assumptions. The user cares deeply about terminological precision and theoretical fidelity. When in doubt, quote the user's terms back and ask for clarification rather than paraphrasing into generic language.

why_keys.levinas: ETHICAL ground: cross-metathesis as asymmetric encounter with irreducible otherness
why_keys.sartre: PROCESSUAL ground: praxis-process (consummation) / process-praxis (consumption) cycle; practico-inert = gas/fuel, praxistatic = engine
why_keys.dg: TOPOLOGICAL ground: tetravalent assemblage = L-matrix; conjunction (gate) vs connection (creative multiplier) drives 3C
why_keys.emery: EMPIRICAL ground: original L11/L12/L21/L22 from organizational systems theory (1965); Type V vortical = unificity
why_keys.user_framework: Unificity.md sits ABOVE all sources: Unity-Multiplicity-Unificity is the foundational triad from which TAPS, RIP, and Dialectic unfold

recent_decisions.chose: Orientation card approach — MEMORY.md becomes lean (~50 lines) with project essentials only; buffer is single source of truth for theoretical content
recent_decisions.why: 228-line MEMORY.md duplicated 40+ lines of concepts and 60+ lines of forward notes already in concept_map. Double token spend when both loaded.

recent_decisions.what: MEMORY.md integration mode for new users
recent_decisions.chose: Three-option choice at first-run: full (restructure to orientation card), minimal (add pointer only), none (independent operation)
recent_decisions.why: Respects user sovereignty. Some users have personal notes they don't want restructured.

recent_decisions.chose: Handoff-only, at full-scan threshold only, max 10 lines per cycle, warm entry remains source of truth
recent_decisions.why: Autosave trigger too expensive. 200-line MEMORY.md cap too tight for aggressive promotion. Full-scan threshold is natural moment.

recent_decisions.chose: Update MEMORY.md status one-liner from active_work at every handoff (new Step 11)
recent_decisions.why: Standalone sessions see stale status otherwise. One-line edit, negligible cost.

instance_notes.from: the instance that reviewed v3 and built MEMORY.md integration (v4 tooling session)
remarks: This was a META session — no theoretical work, no code changes. All changes were to the handoff/onhand skill system and MEMORY.md. The simulator is untouched.
The user thinks architecturally about the tool itself, not just the project. Treat /handoff and /onhand as first-class engineering artifacts.
MEMORY.md went from 228 lines (overflowing the 200-line auto-load cap) to 41 lines. Every removed line has a home in the buffer's concept_map or convergence_web. Zero content loss.
Global skills are at ~/.claude/skills/. Project skills are at repo/.claude/skills/. Global = process, project = structure. Don't confuse them.
The project handoff skill overrides warm max to 800 lines (global default 500) because concept_map + convergence_web are large and non-migratable.
The previous instance's instance_notes (from the Stage 3B builder) are excellent — still fully applicable. Read the cold-layer dialogue_trace too.
The .claude/skills/resume/ directory in the repo is the OLD name before rename to 'onhand'. May need cleanup.
open_questions: The promotion check needs a tracking mechanism for 'pointer-loaded in 3+ consecutive sessions' — the spec doesn't define where this counter lives. Needs design.
Should the distill skill get a MEMORY.md-aware step? Currently writes to buffer only. No path for fundamental concepts to reach the orientation card.
MEMORY.md lives outside the repo (~/.claude/projects/). If user switches machines, it's lost. Should the buffer store a backup?
The user mentioned a previous instance capped out mid-discussion about redundancy. That instance had additional context about what content belongs where. Check if it left any trace.

natural_summary: Meta-tooling session: reviewed v3 skill system, resolved MEMORY.md/buffer redundancy by trimming MEMORY.md to 41-line orientation card and adding automatic integration support (Step 0f in onhand, Step 11 sync in handoff, promotion check at full-scan threshold). No simulator code or theoretical content changed. 345 tests passing. Next: R&B deep review, then TASKS.md and Stage 3C planning.
